description: The table: the flat surface the household gathers at. Built with the 'wo' of 'wolea' (wood) settling onto flat 'pa'. Meals, work, and talk all land here; it is the most social piece of wood in the house.
sound_symbolism: 'wo' carries the wood it is made of, 'ne' levels it, and 'pa' is the flat it offers. The stresses sit low and steady, like the thing.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'miona wonepa pilewa.' (The person makes a table.) 'nuora mua wonepa nai.' (The food is on the table.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The table is where the household faces itself daily. Most of a family's peace is negotiated across one.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The board and trestle outlived every generation that ate at it. Households were counted by their table as much as by their roof.

semantic_domains.physical: The social flat of the house
